items.title: Mobile GP service and after-hours care to end because of Tasmanian health cuts

children.content: Primary care providers being forced to cut services after losing Tasmanian government funding say the result will be added strain on "pressure cooker" emergency departments, which could see them "explode".

gallery.altText: A doctor using a stethoscope on a patient.

thumbnail.altText: A doctor using a stethoscope on a patient.

items.title: Premier's request for greyhound phase-out bill review rejected by committee

image.altText: Greyhounds racing.

children.content: A parliamentary committee has rejected a request to review a bill which would phase out Tasmania's greyhound racing industry. It follows new data which shows 19 ex-racing greyhounds have died in Tasmania this year.

items.title: 'There's one kid in every classroom': Teaching children to navigate grief

children.content: One in 20 Australians under the age of 18 has lost a parent, but families say grief services for children are limited. A national program teaching children how to navigate grief is trying to change that.

image.altText: A young boy being carried on the back of his mother.

items.title: When the bushfire came, this couple turned on their roof sprinkler and left

children.content: Preparing for an ember attack could extend your home's bushfire survivability rate to above 90 per cent or even closer to 100 per cent, a fire scientist says.

image.altText: Aerial perspective of a coastal home spinkler system on roof surrounded by burned trees under a blue sky.

items.title: 'Shocked' water scientist sounds alarm over $300m irrigation plan

children.content: A $300 million irrigation scheme will supply 37.2 gigalitres of water to farming areas near Hobart from 2030. A scientists fears the taxpayer-funded scheme could put a major river at risk.

image.altText: She stands by the river with a red silky scarf around her neck

items.title: 'Bushwalkers have died attempting this track': Instagram post glosses over hike's dangers

children.content: It looks beautiful in the Instagram post with blue skies and picturesque tarns carved by glaciers. But read further and the multi-day Tasmanian hike is considered one of Australia's most challenging bushwalks. In fact, it can be deadly.

image.altText: Tourism Australia post

items.title: 'I thought the plane was going down': 10 injured on Cathay Pacific flight

children.content: An Australian passenger aboard a flight from Brisbane to Hong Kong says he thought he was going to die after the aircraft suddenly plunged mid-air.

Items like juice tetra packs, liquid and other food items strewn on the ground.

items.title: How a container of hot chocolate put the spotlight on airport security

children.content: A security scare that shut down a Victorian airport last week was found to be a false alarm.

image.altText: Passengers line up for security screening at the airport.

items.title: ASX 200 rises after Dow closes at record high, oil falls under $US100 — as it happened

children.content: The Australian share market has begun the new week in positive territory after the Dow Jones posted new records. Meanwhile, the price of Brent crude has dropped under $US100 a barrel. Look back on our business blog.

image.altText: ASX board viewed behind a person's head walking past window

items.title: Trump says a deal is close. Here are the sticking points still dividing the US and Iran

children.content: A possible US-Iran agreement is taking shape, but the toughest issues are still unsettled, including reopening the Strait of Hormuz, curbing Tehran's nuclear ambitions, and ending regional fighting.

articles.title: Trump's deal-making leaves Iran in the driver's seat and Israel more vulnerable

children.content: Israel goes from looking like the new regional hegemon to looking like it is under greater threat now than it has in years, while Iran now has room to rebuild its proxies.

image.altText: Trump purses his lips as he walks outside the White House

articles.title: With an Iran deal looming, Republicans are turning on each other

children.content: The US president says he "won't be rushed" to announce the details, but already critics and Iran are suggesting he has lost the negotiations

image.altText: Man with Jerusalem in the background

gallery.altText: A photo of a shipping vessel carrying cargo containers in the Strait of Hormuz.

thumbnail.altText: A photo of a shipping vessel carrying cargo containers in the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: Man jailed for fatally amputating man's foot using circular saw

children.content: Kalman Tal's loved ones have told of their distress at how the 66-year-old's leg was amputated in a public park. Farm worker John Yalu has today been sentenced for manslaughter.

image.altText: A selfie image of a man wearing a black top on a tropical farm

items.title: Push for law change that could keep Baden-Clay behind bars for longer

children.content: The possible release of wife killer Gerard Baden-Clay next year could be pushed back by up to a decade under proposed changes to Queensland's parole laws.

image.altText: Gerard Baden-Clay

items.title: China launches astronauts into space amid new race to Moon

children.content: China has launched three astronauts into space, including one who will stay for a year for a study of the long-term impacts on a human in orbit, ahead of Beijing's goal of a crewed Moon landing by 2030.

image.altText: China's Shenzhou-23 takes off with fire and light

gallery.altText: A white space ship launching alongside a vertical control tower, above a bright orange ball of flame and smoke cloud.

thumbnail.altText: A white space ship launching alongside a vertical control tower, above a bright orange ball of flame and smoke cloud.

items.title: Aboriginal community workers say diphtheria response 'heartbreaking'

children.content: Residents and service providers in the remote community of Yuendumu, where there are reports of dozens of diphtheria cases, say the NT government has not done enough to keep people safe.

image.altText: a cow standing near a fence with a hill in the background

items.title: Travis Head and wife Jess cop social media abuse after IPL on-field incident

children.content: Australian cricketer Travis Head and his wife Jess Head have their Instagram accounts blasted with vile comments after a series of tense on-field exchanges between Head and Indian star Virat Kohli in an Indian Premier League match.

image.altText: Australian batsman Travis head looks at his bat while Indian fielder Virat Kohli smiles and shouts.

children.content: Mix up your mince menu with these versatile and affordable meals for the whole family.

children.content: Classic Australian swearwords and insults are becoming less popular with younger generations. What does losing them mean for our culture?

children.content: Plus the rom-com of yore returns in Finding Emily, the farmyard mystery that's been delighting audiences, and a gory Hollywood video game adaptation filmed on the Gold Coast.

image.altText: A group of young woman laughing at a restaurant table.

items.title: Housing minister says Waterloo community 'not functional' as demolition looms

children.content: Rose Jackson made the comment on radio as workers began fencing off 150 public homes to be demolished to make way for a mixed-tenure development.

items.title: Surfing event in NZ issued 'code red' after animal attacks photographer

children.content: The New Zealand Pro World Surf League is put on hold after what is believed to be a seal attacked a photographer.

image.altText: A surfer is taken out of the water by a jet ski.

items.title: 'We're taking him home': Aboriginal stockman's remains repatriated

children.content: An Aboriginal stockman whose remains have been held by the South Australian Museum for six decades will this week be returned to Country in a remote national park.

image.altText: The start of a repatriation process at the SA Museum.

items.title: Australian activists detained from Gaza flotilla return home

children.content: Most of the 11 Australian activists on the Gaza-bound Global Sumud Flotilla have returned home after being detained by Israel.

image.altText: A young woman wearing a keffiyeh and a puffer jacket looks at the camera with a serious expression

items.title: 'I did it': Parramatta council official fronts corruption watchdog

children.content: During her third day on the witness stand, Roxanne Thornton told the Independent Commission Against Corruption (ICAC) she was responsible for hiring processes that were "extremely flawed".

image.altText: Roxanne Thornton in a black blazer, purple scarf, green glasses and short cropped ash blonde hair, speaking.

items.title: Australia's opening times are world leading and researchers say it's a strength

children.content: Australia has a "distinctive strength" when it comes to early trading, but more knowledge is needed for businesses to fully capitalise on it.

items.title: Data shows why Australians are stuck and not switching jobs or starting businesses

children.content: People are less mobile, less dynamic and more risk-averse than we used to be, a series of economic indicators shows.

children.content: Paralympic gold medallist Nikki Ayers reflects on a career that saw her face ableism, homophobia and sexism.

items.title: The migration maths behind the housing crisis is not what politicians claim

children.content: Arthur Calwell argued migration was essential to national survival. Today, the Coalition says it should depend on how many homes get built, but the economics are far from straightforward.

items.title: Vegemite banned, Tim Tams welcome: Baseballer brings Aussie flavour to US

children.content: As a teenager, Curtis Mead had to choose between an iconically Australian sport and America's pastime. He's now hitting home runs in Major League Baseball.

image.altText: Curtis Mead on the field during a baseball game.

children.content: Football Australia employees are asked to use three words to describe the culture at the organisation, and the results are damning.
